tasks.description:
Gut useShotGroups inference engine and remove isPinned from ShotGroup interface.

File: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts`

1. Remove `isPinned` from `ShotGroup` interface (line 17).
2. Remove `MAX_GAP_SECONDS` constant (line 7), `flushGroup` helper (lines 31-35), `sortActions` helper (lines 37-39).
3. Remove `meta`, `registry`, `finalVideoShotIds` parameters from signature. New signature: `useShotGroups(rows, shots, pinnedShotGroups)`.
4. Remove `clipGenerationIds` map construction (lines 54-68).
5. Remove entire inference loop: shot iteration, imageIndexByGenerationId, gap detection, flushGroup calls (lines 99-187).
6. Remove deduplication and pin-suppression logic (lines 189-213).
7. Remove all `console.log` debug statements (lines 63, 114, 118, 121).
8. Remove `isPinned: true` from pinned group objects (line 92) since field no longer exists.
9. Remove `pinnedClipIdSets` (line 97) — only used for suppression.
10. Remove early return guarded by `!shots?.length` (lines 99-101) — not needed since inference is gone; the useMemo just returns pinnedGroups directly.
11. Update useMemo dependency array to `[rows, shots, pinnedShotGroups]`.
12. Remove unused imports: `ClipMeta`, `AssetRegistry`.

Final hook body: build shotNameById map, map pinnedShotGroups to ShotGroup[], return. Should be ~40 lines.

Update useShotGroups tests to remove inference tests and update pinned tests.

File: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts`

1. Remove inference-only tests (lines 42-132): 'adjacent clips', 'gap exceeds threshold', 'order disagrees', 'separate groups different shots', 'single clip', 'cross-track', 'ignores clips without generation ids'.
2. Remove inferred/pinned interaction tests (lines 151-251): 'suppresses inferred subset', 'does not suppress different cluster', 'merges pinned and inferred'.
3. Keep `getShotColor` deterministic test (lines 134-137).
4. Keep and update 'returns pinned groups' test (lines 197-218): remove `isPinned: true` from expected output, update call to new signature `useShotGroups(rows, shots, pinnedShotGroups)`. The test currently passes `meta`, `registry`, `shots`, `undefined`, `pinnedShotGroups` — change to just `rows, shots, pinnedShotGroups`.
5. Remove `buildMeta` and `buildRegistry` helpers if no remaining tests use them.
6. Add tests: (a) returns empty array when pinnedShotGroups is undefined, (b) filters out pinned group when trackId doesn't match any row, (c) filters out dead clipIds that aren't in row actions.

Simplify TimelineEditor: remove resolveFinalVideoShotIds, finalVideoShotIds, and update useShotGroups call.

File: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx`

1. Remove `resolveFinalVideoShotIds` export function (lines 126-159).
2. Remove `import type { ShotFinalVideo }` (line 37) — no longer needed.
3. Change destructure at line 272 from `{ finalVideoMap, dismissedFinalVideoIds, dismissFinalVideo }` to `{ finalVideoMap, dismissFinalVideo }`. Keep both — finalVideoMap is used by useSwitchToFinalVideo and will be passed to TimelineCanvas; dismissFinalVideo is called in onShotGroupSwitchToFinalVideo handler (line 644-646).
4. Remove `finalVideoShotIds` useMemo (lines 322-330).
5. Update `useShotGroups` call (lines 331-338) to new signature: `useShotGroups(data?.rows ?? [], shots, data?.config.pinnedShotGroups)`.
6. Replace `finalVideoShotIds={finalVideoShotIds}` prop on `<TimelineCanvas>` (line 632) with `finalVideoMap={finalVideoMap}`.
7. Remove `onShotGroupPin` prop (lines 636-638) from `<TimelineCanvas>` JSX.
8. Remove `onShotGroupDismissFinalVideo` prop (lines 652-656) from `<TimelineCanvas>` JSX.
9. Keep `onShotGroupSwitchToFinalVideo` handler (lines 642-648) — it still calls dismissFinalVideo + handleSwitchToFinalVideo.
10. Check if `EMPTY_CLIP_META` and `EMPTY_ASSET_REGISTRY` constants (lines 39-40) are still referenced elsewhere in the file before deleting.

Simplify TimelineCanvas: remove isPinned distinction, replace finalVideoShotIds with finalVideoMap, remove dead props.

File: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`

1. Remove `isPinned` from `PositionedShotGroup` interface (line 68).
2. In `TimelineCanvasProps` (line 101): replace `finalVideoShotIds?: Set<string>` with `finalVideoMap?: Map<string, unknown>`. Remove `onShotGroupPin` prop (line 107). Remove `onShotGroupDismissFinalVideo` prop (line 109).
3. Update destructured props (lines 335, 341, 343): remove `finalVideoShotIds`, add `finalVideoMap`, remove `onShotGroupPin`, remove `onShotGroupDismissFinalVideo`.
4. Positioned group computation (line 468): remove `isPinned: group.isPinned === true`. Change line 470 from `finalVideoShotIds?.has(group.shotId)` to `finalVideoMap?.has(group.shotId) ?? false`.
5. Update useMemo deps (line 478): replace `finalVideoShotIds` with `finalVideoMap`.
6. Remove `isPinned` from `openShotGroupMenu` type (line 483).
7. Border styling (line 777): always use `'border-2 border-solid'` — remove dashed conditional.
8. `data-shot-group-kind` (line 779): always `'pinned'`.
9. Border color (lines 786-788): always use 60% opacity.
10. Top bar (lines 805-812): always use `group.top - 18`, height `18`, 78% opacity.
11. Shot name span (line 815): remove `group.isPinned &&` conditional — always show.
12. Remove `onShotGroupPin` and `onShotGroupDismissFinalVideo` from ShotGroupContextMenu rendering props.

Simplify ShotGroupContextMenu: remove isPinned checks, Pin action, and Dismiss action.

File: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx`

1. Remove `isPinned` from `ShotGroupMenuState` (line 13).
2. Remove `onPinGroup` prop (line 25) and `onDismissFinalVideo` prop (line 27) from interface.
3. Remove from destructured props: `onPinGroup`, `onDismissFinalVideo`.
4. `pinActions` (lines 46-63): remove the `!menu.isPinned && onPinGroup` branch (Pin as Shot Group action). Keep only the Unpin action, simplify condition to just `onUnpinGroup ? { ... } : null`.
5. `finalVideoActions` (line 64): change condition from `menu.hasFinalVideo && (!menu.isPinned || menu.mode === 'images')` to `menu.hasFinalVideo && menu.mode !== 'video'` (show switch-to-video when in images mode or no mode set). Remove the `onDismissFinalVideo` 'Dismiss reminder' action entry (lines 74-76).
6. `imageActions` (line 79): change from `menu.isPinned && menu.mode === 'video'` to just `menu.mode === 'video'`.

Clean up useFinalVideoAvailable: remove module-level dismissedFinalVideoIds Set export.

File: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useFinalVideoAvailable.ts`

1. Remove the module-level `dismissedFinalVideoIds` export (line 10). Keep it as a non-exported module-level Set since `dismissFinalVideo` callback still writes to it (line 18).
2. Remove `dismissedFinalVideoIds` from the hook's return value (line 24).
3. Keep `finalVideoMap` and `dismissFinalVideo` — both are still used.
4. Check if `ShotFinalVideo` re-export (line 8) is still needed by any file after T3 and T7 remove the imports. If not needed, remove it.

Update TimelineEditor.test.ts and TimelineCanvas.test.tsx to remove references to deleted symbols (FLAG-001).

File: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts`
1. Remove `resolveFinalVideoShotIds` from import (line 6). Remove `ShotFinalVideo` import (line 3).
2. Delete the entire `describe('resolveFinalVideoShotIds', ...)` block (lines 117-172).

File: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`
1. Remove `finalVideoShotIds` from render helper params type (line 46) and JSX prop (line 85).
2. Remove `onShotGroupPin` from params type (line 47) and JSX prop (line 86).
3. Update test 'renders suggested groups dashed and keeps pinned group labels visible' (lines 303-331): Rewrite to test that all groups render with solid border and always show the shot name label. Remove `isPinned` from test shot group objects. Remove assertion for `border-dashed`.
4. Update test 'shows pin and switch-to-video actions for suggested groups with a final video' (lines 333-364): Replace with a test for pinned groups showing 'Switch to Final Video' and 'Unpin' but NOT 'Pin as Shot Group'. Replace `finalVideoShotIds` with `finalVideoMap`. Remove `onShotGroupPin`.
5. Update test 'shows unpin and switch-to-images actions for pinned video groups' (lines 366-398): Remove `isPinned: true` from group, replace `finalVideoShotIds` with `finalVideoMap`.

Run all tests, fix TypeScript errors, and verify.

1. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to catch type errors across the codebase. Fix any broken references to removed symbols (isPinned, finalVideoShotIds, onShotGroupPin, onShotGroupDismissFinalVideo, resolveFinalVideoShotIds, dismissedFinalVideoIds export).
2. Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts` to verify the updated hook tests pass.
3. Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts` to verify TimelineEditor tests pass.
4. Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` to verify TimelineCanvas tests pass.
5. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run.
6. Verify no `console.log` statements remain in useShotGroups.ts.
7. Verify useShotGroups.ts is under 60 lines.
8. Quick grep for any remaining references to removed symbols across the video-editor directory.

watch_items: EMPTY_CLIP_META and EMPTY_ASSET_REGISTRY constants in TimelineEditor.tsx may still be used by other code in the file (e.g., resolveWaveformAudioSrc) — check before deleting

ShotFinalVideo re-export from useFinalVideoAvailable.ts is imported by TimelineEditor.test.ts line 3 — if the resolveFinalVideoShotIds tests are deleted, this import goes away, but verify no other file imports ShotFinalVideo from useFinalVideoAvailable
The finalVideoMap passed to TimelineCanvas needs to support `.has()` — typed as Map<string, unknown> is sufficient since only .has() is called
dismissFinalVideo is still called inside onShotGroupSwitchToFinalVideo handler (TimelineEditor line 644-646) — keep the dismiss infrastructure even though the Dismiss Reminder menu action is removed

The floating final-video badge button (TimelineCanvas ~line 824) renders for groups with hasFinalVideo — this should still work since hasFinalVideo is now derived from finalVideoMap instead of finalVideoShotIds
DEBT: timeline-shot-group-cleanup — this task directly resolves FLAG-001 by updating TimelineEditor.test.ts and TimelineCanvas.test.tsx

The pinGroup/unpinGroup imports from usePinnedShotGroups (TimelineEditor line 31) — pinGroup is no longer passed to TimelineCanvas but may still be used elsewhere in TimelineEditor; check before removing
Assumption: 'Dismiss reminder' context menu action is removed entirely (per FLAG-002 resolution and plan assumption) since it was for inferred group badge suppression

sense_checks.question: Does useShotGroups now return ONLY pinned groups with no inference logic, no gap detection, no image-order checking? Is the file under 60 lines?
Are all inference-related tests removed, pinned tests updated to new signature without isPinned, and new edge-case tests added for missing track / dead clipIds?
Is resolveFinalVideoShotIds fully deleted, finalVideoShotIds useMemo removed, useShotGroups called with simplified 3-arg signature, and finalVideoMap passed to TimelineCanvas instead?
Does TimelineCanvas render all groups with solid border-2, always show the shot name label, derive hasFinalVideo from finalVideoMap, and have no isPinned references remaining?
Is 'Pin as Shot Group' action removed, 'Dismiss reminder' action removed, and remaining actions (Unpin, Switch to Final Video, Switch to Images) no longer gated on isPinned?
Is the module-level dismissedFinalVideoIds Set no longer exported, while dismissFinalVideo callback and finalVideoMap are preserved?
Do TimelineEditor.test.ts and TimelineCanvas.test.tsx compile without references to deleted symbols (resolveFinalVideoShotIds, isPinned, finalVideoShotIds, onShotGroupPin)?
Do tsc --noEmit and all three test files pass cleanly? Are there zero remaining references to removed symbols in the video-editor directory?

meta_commentary:
Execution order: T1 first (gut the hook), then T2+T3+T6 can run in parallel (tests, TimelineEditor, useFinalVideoAvailable), then T4 (TimelineCanvas depends on T3's prop changes), then T5 (context menu depends on T4), then T7 (test updates need final interfaces), then T8 (verify everything).

Key judgment calls:
- `EMPTY_CLIP_META` and `EMPTY_ASSET_REGISTRY` in TimelineEditor.tsx: check if resolveWaveformAudioSrc or other code still uses them before deleting. The `EMPTY_ASSET_GENERATION_MAP` constant (line 41) may also be safe to remove — check usage.
- `finalVideoMap` type in TimelineCanvas props: use `Map<string, unknown>` since we only call `.has()`. The actual `ShotFinalVideo` type doesn't need to leak into TimelineCanvas.
- The `onShotGroupSwitchToFinalVideo` handler in TimelineEditor still calls `dismissFinalVideo(finalVideo.id)` before switching — this is fine to keep. The dismiss state prevents the badge from reappearing after switching, which is still useful behavior.
- For finalVideoActions condition in ShotGroupContextMenu, use `menu.mode !== 'video'` rather than `menu.mode === 'images'` so groups with no mode set (undefined) still show the switch-to-video option.
- FLAG-002 resolution: remove 'Dismiss reminder' entirely since it only made sense for auto-detected badges on inferred groups.
- The module-level `dismissedFinalVideoIds` Set in useFinalVideoAvailable.ts should be kept internally (not exported) since `dismissFinalVideo` still writes to it — just stop exporting it.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Simplify useShotGroups hook — gut inference engine, remove isPinned, simplify signature
Step 2: Update useShotGroups tests — remove inference tests, update pinned tests, add edge cases
Step 3: Simplify TimelineEditor — remove resolveFinalVideoShotIds, finalVideoShotIds, update useShotGroups call, pass finalVideoMap
Step 4: Simplify TimelineCanvas — remove isPinned visual distinction, replace finalVideoShotIds with finalVideoMap, remove dead props
Step 5: Simplify ShotGroupContextMenu — remove isPinned checks, Pin action, Dismiss action
Step 6: Clean up useFinalVideoAvailable — remove module-level dismissedFinalVideoIds export
Step 7: Remove dead props from TimelineEditor to TimelineCanvas interface (onShotGroupPin, onShotGroupDismissFinalVideo)
FLAG-001: Update TimelineEditor.test.ts and TimelineCanvas.test.tsx for deleted symbols
Validation: run tests, tsc, verify no remaining dead references

validation.completeness_notes: All 7 plan steps covered. Step 7 (dead props cleanup) is folded into T3 and T4 since the prop removal happens naturally when editing those files. FLAG-001 explicitly addressed in T7. FLAG-002 (Dismiss reminder inconsistency) resolved in T5 by removing the action entirely.
